speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Oft have I heard that grief softens the mind

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And makes it fearful and degenerate.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Think therefore on revenge, and cease to weep.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: But who can cease to weep and look on this?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Here may his head lie on my throbbing breast,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: But where’s the body that I should embrace?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: What answer makes your Grace to the rebels’ supplication?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I’ll send some holy bishop to entreat,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For God forbid so many simple souls

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Should perish by the sword! And I myself,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Rather than bloody war shall cut them short,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Will parley with Jack Cade, their general.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But stay, I’ll read it over once again.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Ah, barbarous villains! Hath this lovely face

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Ruled, like a wandering planet, over me,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And could it not enforce them to relent

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That were unworthy to behold the same?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Lord Saye, Jack Cade hath sworn to have thy head.

speaker: SAYE
text: Ay, but I hope your Highness shall have his.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: How now, madam?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Still lamenting and mourning for Suffolk’s death?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I fear me, love, if that I had been dead,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thou wouldst not have mourned so much for me.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: No, my love, I should not mourn, but die for thee.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: How now, what news? Why com’st thou in such haste?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The rebels are in Southwark. Fly, my lord!

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Jack Cade proclaims himself Lord Mortimer,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Descended from the Duke of Clarence’ house,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And calls your Grace usurper, openly,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And vows to crown himself in Westminster.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: His army is a ragged multitude

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Of hinds and peasants, rude and merciless.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Sir Humphrey Stafford and his brother’s death

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Hath given them heart and courage to proceed.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: All scholars, lawyers, courtiers, gentlemen

speaker: MESSENGER
text: They call false caterpillars and intend their death.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O, graceless men, they know not what they do!

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: My gracious lord, retire to Killingworth

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Until a power be raised to put them down.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Ah, were the Duke of Suffolk now alive,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: These Kentish rebels would be soon appeased!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Lord Saye, the traitors hateth thee;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Therefore away with us to Killingworth.

speaker: SAYE
text: So might your Grace’s person be in danger.

speaker: SAYE
text: The sight of me is odious in their eyes;

speaker: SAYE
text: And therefore in this city will I stay

speaker: SAYE
text: And live alone as secret as I may.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: Jack Cade hath gotten London Bridge.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: The citizens fly and forsake their houses.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: The rascal people, thirsting after prey,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: Join with the traitor, and they jointly swear

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: To spoil the city and your royal court.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Then linger not, my lord. Away! Take horse!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Come, Margaret. God, our hope, will succor us.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: My hope is gone, now Suffolk is deceased.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Farewell, my lord. Trust not the Kentish rebels.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Trust nobody, for fear you be betrayed.

speaker: SAYE
text: The trust I have is in mine innocence,

speaker: SAYE
text: And therefore am I bold and resolute.
